title: Chicken Provençal

overview.description: Chicken braised with tomatoes, garlic, olives, anchovies, capers and Provençal herbs.

method: Pat the chicken dry and season lightly with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based casserole over a medium-high heat.
Brown the chicken thighs skin-side down until golden.
Turn and brown the other side briefly.
Remove the chicken from the casserole.
Add the onion and cook until softened.
Cook for 1–2 minutes, stirring, until the anchovies have dissolved into the oil.
Pour in the white wine and scrape the bottom of the casserole to release the browned bits.
Allow the wine to reduce by approximately half.
Add the chopped tomatoes, chicken stock, thyme, oregano and bay leaf.
Return the chicken to the casserole, skin-side up.
Cover and cook for 35–45 minutes until the chicken is tender and cooked through.
Add the olives and capers during the final 10 minutes of cooking.
Taste and adjust the seasoning carefully.
Scatter with fresh parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Provençal cooking is strongly associated with ingredients such as tomatoes, garlic, olives, herbs and olive oil, reflecting the Mediterranean climate and produce of southern France.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How anchovies can add savoury depth without making a dish taste fishy, and how capers and olives balance a rich tomato-based braise.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A Mediterranean-style chicken braise that introduces the children to anchovies and capers in a balanced, approachable dish.
chapman-tips: The anchovies should dissolve into the sauce rather than remain as obvious pieces.
Be careful with added salt because the anchovies, olives and capers are all salty.
Add the olives and capers towards the end so they retain more of their character.
Use a dry white wine that you would be happy to drink.
A piece of crusty bread is particularly good for soaking up the sauce.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in portions. Freeze the chicken and sauce together.
notes.leftovers: Remove the chicken from the bones and use the meat and sauce with pasta, rice or crusty bread.
